name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Delete Command
notes:
This Building Block detects suspicious command line to remove 'exe' or 'dll'

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: frack113
SIGMA Title: Greedy File Deletion Using Del
SIGMA Description: Detects execution of the "del" builtin command to remove files using greedy/wildcard expression. This is often used by malware to delete content of folders that perhaps contains the initial malware infection or to delete evidence.


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Delete Command on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [del or *.exe or /f or /q], Command (custom) contains any of [del or \*.dll or C:\ProgramData\]
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Delete Command on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [del or *.exe or /f or /q], Command (custom) contains any of [del or \*.dll or C:\ProgramData\]
